text.title: Persona Building Agent Gotchas
text.summary: LLM failure modes specific to persona synthesis: invented quotes, demographic stereotypes, PII in transcripts, temperature effects, human-in-the-loop checkpoints.

antipattern.description: Asking an LLM to "write a persona for indie SaaS founders" without supplying transcript data.
antipattern.reason: LLMs will produce plausible-sounding frustrations, quotes, and demographics from training data priors rather than your actual customers. Every persona trait must be grounded in a transcript citation.

antipattern.description: Running synthesis at temperature above 0.3 for clustering and trait extraction.
antipattern.reason: Higher temperature fabricates variation that looks like diverse customer segments. Use temperature 0 for clustering; reserve creative phrasing only for the optional characteristic quote field.

antipattern.description: Feeding 30+ interview transcripts into one synthesis call.
antipattern.reason: Long-context dumps cause the model to collapse to averages and lose outliers. Use map-reduce: cluster per transcript, merge cluster summaries, synthesise the merged summaries.

antipattern.description: Including demographic fields (age, city, income) without 5+ transcript agreements.
antipattern.reason: LLMs encode demographic stereotypes from training data. "32-year-old freelance designer in Brooklyn" will appear plausibly even when none of your interviews confirm it. Strip demographics from the synthesis prompt unless the data supports them.

rule.statement: Instruct the synthesis agent: "Include no demographic field unless 5 or more transcripts contain explicit agreement on that dimension."
rule.rationale: Enforces an evidence bar that prevents demographic stereotypes from leaking through model priors.

rule.statement: Run password-scrubber-agent or equivalent before committing any interview transcript to the repository. Reference participants by ID (P07), never name.
rule.rationale: Interview notes contain names, emails, Slack handles, and employer names. PII crossing repo boundaries is a compliance and trust violation, especially for EU-based interviewees under GDPR.

antipattern.description: Always feeding transcripts in the same order to the synthesis agent.
antipattern.reason: LLMs exhibit recency and primacy bias; the first and last transcripts in a batch disproportionately shape the output. Shuffle transcript order between runs; if the persona changes drastically, the data is too thin for a stable finding.

list.item: Before committing persona to repo: human reviews all citations; any uncited trait is flagged HALLUCINATION and removed.
Before using persona to drive ad spend: human re-interviews 2 current customers to confirm the persona still holds.
Do not let an agent autonomously update the persona doc on a schedule. Drift detection can be automated; rewrites must not be.
